---
title: "Centipede Grass Orchid (Zeuxine strateumatica)"
slug: "zeuxine-strateumatica"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354631027/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Centipede Grass Orchid"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Asparagales"
tax_family: "Orchidaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Asparagales", "Orchidaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354631027/medium.jpg" alt="Centipede Grass Orchid" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/dbond">
dbond</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200947541">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Asparagales
-   **Family:** Orchidaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=124426)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in lawns, pastures, and other disturbed grassy areas.

## Natural history notes

A small terrestrial orchid characterized by its low rosette of leaves and slender spike of tiny white flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in lawns, pastures, and other disturbed grassy areas, often in moist soils. It typically blooms during the cooler months and can appear in large numbers under suitable conditions.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/124426-Zeuxine-strateumatica)
-   [Local profile](https://goorchids.northamericanorchidcenter.org/species/zeuxine/strateumatica/)
